From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Fourth-level administrative divisions of Italy

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This article **relies largely or entirely on a [single source](/wiki/Wikipedia:Articles_with_a_single_source "Wikipedia:Articles with a single source")**. Relevant discussion may be found on the [talk page](/wiki/Talk:Frazione "Talk:Frazione"). Please help [improve this article](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Frazione&action=edit) by [introducing citations to additional sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). *Find sources:* ["Frazione"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Frazione%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Frazione%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Frazione%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Frazione%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Frazione%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Frazione%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(December 2024)* |

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a0/Bolcapanorama.jpg/330px-Bolcapanorama.jpg)](/wiki/File:Bolcapanorama.jpg)

View of [Bolca](/wiki/Bolca "Bolca"), a *frazione* of the *comune* of [Vestenanova](/wiki/Vestenanova "Vestenanova"), [Veneto](/wiki/Veneto "Veneto").

A ***frazione*** (pl.: *frazioni*, cognate with English *[fraction](/wiki/Fraction "Fraction")*) is a type of subdivision of a *[comune](/wiki/Comune "Comune")* ('municipality') in Italy, often a small village or hamlet outside the main town, similar to a [suburb](/wiki/Suburb "Suburb") or [exurb](/wiki/Exurb "Exurb"). Most *frazioni* were created during the [Fascist era](/wiki/Fascist_Italy "Fascist Italy") (1922–1943) as a way to consolidate territorial subdivisions in the country.

In the autonomous region of the [Aosta Valley](/wiki/Aosta_Valley "Aosta Valley"), a *frazione* is officially called *hameau* in French. In [South Tyrol](/wiki/South_Tyrol "South Tyrol"), a *frazione* is called *Fraktion* in German and *frazion* in [Ladin](/wiki/Ladin_language "Ladin language").

## Description

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Frazione&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Description")]

The term *frazioni* refers to the villages or hamlets that often make up a *comune* in rural Italian areas.[[1]](#cite_note-1) Subdivision of a *comune* is optional; some *comuni* have no *frazioni*, but others have several dozen. The *comune* usually has the same name of the *[capoluogo](/wiki/Comune#Subdivisions "Comune")*, but not always, in which case it is called a *comune sparso*.

In practice, most *frazioni* are small [villages](/wiki/Village "Village") or [hamlets](/wiki/Hamlet_(place) "Hamlet (place)"), occasionally just a clump of houses. Not every hamlet is classified as a *frazione*; those that are not are often referred to as *[località](/wiki/Localit%C3%A0 "Località")*, for example, in the telephone book. In some cases, *frazioni* are more populous than the *capoluogo* of the *comune*. Due to unusual circumstances or to the depopulation of the *capoluogo*, the town hall and its administrative functions can move to one of the *frazioni*: the *comune* still retains the name of the *capoluogo*.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Frazione&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: History")]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This section **does not [cite](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citing_sources "Wikipedia:Citing sources") any [sources](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this section](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Frazione "Special:EditPage/Frazione") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). Unsourced material may be challenged and [removed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability#Burden_of_evidence "Wikipedia:Verifiability"). *(December 2024)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

Historically, many *frazioni* came into being during the Fascist era, when a major effort was made to consolidate and rationalize the territorial subdivisions of the country. Sometimes, a *frazione* represents a former *comune* that was believed to be no longer viable.

Until 2000, the central government established the *frazioni* and defined their borders, except in the case of the five [autonomous regions](/wiki/Autonomous_administrative_division "Autonomous administrative division") (see [regions of Italy](/wiki/Regions_of_Italy "Regions of Italy")), where this was controlled at the regional level. By the Legislative Decree 267/2000 to implement amendments to Title V of the Italian Constitution, the individual *comuni* now define the *frazioni* within their borders.

## Officers

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Frazione&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Officers")]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This section **does not [cite](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citing_sources "Wikipedia:Citing sources") any [sources](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this section](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Frazione "Special:EditPage/Frazione") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). Unsourced material may be challenged and [removed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability#Burden_of_evidence "Wikipedia:Verifiability"). *(December 2024)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

Under the former legislation, a *frazione* had the option of having a submayor (*prosindaco*), who was appointed by the [mayor](/wiki/Mayor "Mayor") (*sindaco*) of the *comune*, often on the recommendation of deliberative bodies such as the communal council (*consiglio*) or the *giunta*, or as a result of a petition by enough residents of the *frazione* involved; although there was no official provision for *frazioni* to group together with the appointment of a single *prosindaco*, this did happen quite often. Under current law, however, Article 54 of the d.lgs. 267/2000 provides that a mayor may delegate mayoral functions at the *frazione* level to a councillor of the *comune*.

In many *comuni*, in addition to their advisory function, the *frazioni* have their clerks and recorders of deeds, but they do not maintain their own civil records.
